description_long: This futuristic airplane features a sleek, angular design with a dark metallic body accented by gold and turquoise highlights. It has four prominent engine nacelles mounted at the ends of swept, low-mounted wings, each with visible intakes and exhausts. The central fuselage is compact and geometric, with a forward-placed, enclosed cockpit that appears to have a tinted canopy. The tail section is split into two vertical stabilizers, each extending from the rear of the wing struts, giving it a distinctive H-tail configuration. The surface is covered in detailed panel lines, insignias, and technical markings, suggesting a high-tech or military application. No visible landing gear is present, and the overall form is aerodynamic and aggressive, suitable for rapid maneuvering in urban airspace.
description: A futuristic, angular airplane with four engine nacelles, a dark metallic body with gold accents, and a split H-tail. The cockpit is forward-mounted and enclosed, with detailed surface markings and a high-tech appearance.
description_view_0: Front view shows the aggressive, angular nose, forward cockpit, and the symmetrical arrangement of four engine nacelles at the wing tips.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the low-mounted, swept wings, the compact fuselage, and the vertical stabilizers extending rearward from the wings.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the split H-tail configuration, the exhausts of the engines, and the broad wingspan with detailed markings on the upper surfaces.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors view 1, showing the same wing and engine arrangement, with clear visibility of the tail structure and fuselage profile.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, swept configuration with pronounced anhedral angle and engine nacelles mounted at the wing tips; no visible winglets.
attribute_engine_type: Four jet engines, each integrated into a nacelle at the end of each wing.
attribute_tail_design: H-tail configuration with twin vertical stabilizers extending from the rear of the wing struts.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single canopy, likely single or dual-seat based on size.
